tavily.answer: In 2025, the number of internal displacements caused by violence or conflict reached a record high, surpassing those caused by natural disasters for the first time, according to a report by the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre. The United Nations refugee agency reported that nearly 118 million people were displaced by conflict and persecution in the same year, marking a decline from previous years but still alarmingly high. In Malaysia, there have been no specific reports of internal displacements due to conflict or violence, but the country has been involved in addressing maritime issues, including the exploitation of jurisdictional gaps by Iranian-linked tankers for oil transfers. Malaysia's robust fintech ecosystem continues to develop, supported by strong regulation and high digital adoption.
